summary: The diff implements a substantial portion of the build request. The backend Motoko actor, frontend pages, components, hooks, and theme system are all present. However, there are several gaps and deviations: the theme CSS is embedded in index.css rather than a dedicated theme.css file; the backend imports from multiple authorization/approval module files which may violate the single-actor constraint; a ProfileSetupModal with name/email setup is added without being requested; extra inventory fields (price, supplier, expirationDate, barcode) are added beyond the spec; and the exact token names required by REQ-2 are not used.

missing_requirements.requirement: Define a single CSS theme file (e.g., frontend/src/styles/theme.css) using CSS custom properties for all color tokens. Import this file globally.
missing_requirements.reason: The theme tokens are defined inside frontend/src/index.css rather than a dedicated standalone theme.css file. No frontend/src/styles/theme.css file exists in the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: Tokens must cover the exact names: background-primary, background-secondary, text-primary, text-muted, text-disabled, border-color, accent-primary, accent-primary-hover, accent-foreground.
missing_requirements.reason: The CSS uses Shadcn/Tailwind token names (--background, --foreground, --primary, etc.) rather than the exact token names required by the spec (--background-primary, --background-secondary, --text-primary, --text-muted, --text-disabled, --border-color, --accent-primary, --accent-primary-hover, --accent-foreground).

missing_requirements.requirement: All Motoko logic must reside in a single actor in backend/main.mo per the build constraints.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend/main.mo imports logic from separate module files (authorization/access-control, authorization/MixinAuthorization, user-approval/approval), which may violate the constraint that all logic reside in a single actor file.

hallucinated_features.description: ProfileSetupModal component that prompts users to enter their full name and email after login, plus saveCallerUserProfile and getCallerUserProfile backend API calls.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not include a profile setup flow or modal for entering name/email. The User data model only specifies email — there is no 'name' field in the spec. The ProfileSetupModal and useSaveCallerUserProfile hook are entirely unspecified.

hallucinated_features.description: Extra InventoryItem fields: price (Float), supplier (Text), expirationDate (?Int), barcode (Text) added to the data model and AddItemModal form.
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec defines InventoryItem with exactly: id, name, sku, category, quantity, unit, lowStockThreshold, description, createdAt, updatedAt. The fields price, supplier, expirationDate, and barcode were not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Access control initialization via _initializeAccessControlWithSecret() and caffeineAdminToken URL parameter handling.
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec does not mention any admin token initialization, secret parameter, or access control seeding mechanism. The useActor.ts hook calls actor._initializeAccessControlWithSecret(adminToken) and reads caffeineAdminToken from URL params, which is an unspecified feature.

hallucinated_features.description: Extra backend functions: requestApproval(), isCallerApproved(), listApprovals(), and setApproval() exposed on the actor beyond the specified API.
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec defines a fixed list of backend functions. These four functions are not among the specified ones.

hallucinated_features.description: UserProfile includes a 'name' field not present in the spec's User data model.
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec's User model defines: principal, email, role, status, createdAt, lastLoginAt. The implemented UserProfile adds a 'name: Text' field which was not requested.
